prompt_latex: An alloy is made by mixing alloy A (copper to zinc ratio $3:3$) and alloy B (copper to zinc ratio $5:2$). How many kilograms of alloy A are needed to make $63$ kg of alloy with copper to zinc ratio $25:17$?
solution_latex: Let $x$ be the kilograms of alloy A. Then $x \cdot \frac{3}{6} + (63-x) \cdot \frac{5}{7} = 63 \cdot \frac{25}{42}$, giving $x = 35$.
